name: Geoffrey Keezer
abstract:
Geoffrey Keezer (born November 20, 1970) is an American jazz pianist. In 2023, he won the Best Instrumental Composition Grammy   for Refuge
Keezer was playing in jazz clubs as a teenager, playing piano for Art Blakey at age 18 and touring with Joshua Redman, Benny Golson and Ray Brown in his 20s.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival New York: Geoff Keezer
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Count Basie Revisited: 1936-1994

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I would certainly say Geoff Keezer... That happened. I think that’s when I did have him. I made sure he sent me a record, and I had some kind of a bio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: And there’s a young pianist named Geoff Keezer... I think we have a decent record. We did the mixing yesterday.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: I was nominated along with Chucho Valdes, Miguel Zenon, Claudio Roditi, and Geoff Keezer, pianist I guess.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: When I first met Geoff, it was out at the University of Idaho at one of the Lionel Hampton concerts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
